sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a history of frequent lower respiratory infections, occurring approximately monthly or every other month, particularly over the past six months. He describes symptoms including persistent cough, mucus production, and low-grade fever. He notes that his symptoms often begin when unloading hay. He denies any personal history of allergies and does not smoke. He reports that no one in his family has a history of allergies either. He mentions ten siblings.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's respiratory rate is 20 breaths per minute, with oxygen saturation at 99% on room air. A fine rale is noted on lung exam. Chest x-ray reveals round glass opacities. Neurological exam is nonfocal.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with hypersensitivity pneumonitis, also known as farmer's lung, likely triggered by exposure to mold and bacteria in hay.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am prescribing an oral steroid course to reduce inflammation in the lungs. I am ordering a CT scan of the lungs to confirm the diagnosis and a pulmonary function test to assess respiratory impairment. I recommend wearing a respirator when moving hay to minimize exposure and prevent further lung damage. Follow up as needed.
